Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. SPENCER H. KELLY, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-438-5928) for defendant Lyft, Inc.
Atty. NICOLE ARIAS, Esq. of Daniels, Fine, Israel, Schonbuch & Lebovits (Tel: 310-556-7900) for defendant Calvin Leong.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

There being no appearance at today's hearing by any contesting party, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted.

Defendant states that at the time of the August 18, 2019 accident, Plaintiff was under the September 30, 2016 terms of service agreement (Coombs Decl., 5:7-8), and in addition, she was also bound by a later January 18, 2021 terms of service agreement (See MPA, fn. 2)-with both governing her use of the Lyft application, website, and technology platform. (Id. at Ex. 2.) Plaintiff states that she did not use the Lyft to order a ride on August 18, 2019, when the Lyft driver ran over her foot. (See Mancillas Decl., 1:22.) Defendant's reply does not refute Plaintiff's claim or offer evidence to the contrary. (See Reply.)

Plaintiff does not raise any argument about the validity of the arbitration agreement, only about the arbitrability of her claim, which under their agreement is for the arbitrator to decide. (Henry Schein, Inc. v. Archer and White Sales, Inc. (2019) 139 S.Ct. 524, 530 [A court may not decide an arbitrability question that the parties have delegated to an arbitrator.].) Thus, because Plaintiff consented to Defendant's terms of service, she is bound to arbitration and the arbitrator will decide if her claim falls within the scope of the agreement. (See Coombs Decl., Ex. 2, Ex. 6.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
